title: erigeron annuus subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Erigeron annuus subsp. septentrionalis, also known as the northern fleabane, is a subspecies of the annual fleabane plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to the northern regions of North America and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and providing relief from fever and cold symptoms. The herbal preparations of Erigeron annuus subsp. septentrionalis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote healing, reduce pain, and boost the immune system.
